claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 3 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 8 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 2 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 2 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit in the UK.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances. Let me start with the basics:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have three children - Alex who is 3, Blake who is 8, and Charlie who is 2. They all live with me here in the UK.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! You have three children - Alex (3), Blake (8), and Charlie (2) - all living with you in the UK.

All three of your children are under 16, which is a good start. Now I need to ask a few more questions:

**Are any of your children (Alex, Blake, or Charlie) working full-time, or are they all not working?** 

(For children this young, they typically wouldn't be working, but I need to confirm this for the assessment.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of them are working - they're all just kids at home with me. Alex and Charlie are too young for school, and Blake is in primary school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for confirming that. 

Now, a couple more questions to complete the assessment:

**Are any of your children receiving benefits in their own right, such as Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit?**

(Again, this is unlikely for children aged 2, 3, and 8, but I need to confirm.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of them receive any benefits in their own name. They're too young for that sort of thing.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good, thank you for confirming that. 

Now I need to ask about your own circumstances:

**Do you normally live in the UK?** Or if not, do any of these apply to you:
- You have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
- You're living abroad as a Crown servant

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - we're all based here in England.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. You mentioned earlier that all three children live with you. Let me confirm:

**Do Alex, Blake, and Charlie all live with you full-time?** Or do any of them spend time living elsewhere (for example, with another parent or family member)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, they all live with me full-time at home.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now an important question:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for any of your children (Alex, Blake, or Charlie)?** For example, another parent or family member?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for any of them. It's just me looking after them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. Now I need to check if there are any special living arrangements for your children:

**Are any of your children (Alex, Blake, or Charlie) currently:**
- In hospital?
- In care (looked after by the local council)?
- Being fostered?

Or are they all living normally at home with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: They're all living normally at home with me. None of them are in hospital, care, or being fostered.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. Just one final question:

**Is the local council paying towards any of your children's accommodation or maintenance costs?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, the council isn't paying anything towards their accommodation or maintenance. I'm covering everything myself.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Excellent News! You ARE Eligible for Child Benefit

Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible for Child Benefit for all three of your children: Alex, Blake, and Charlie.**

### Why you're eligible:

✅ All three children are under 16 years old  
✅ None of them are working or receiving benefits in their own right  
✅ You live in the UK with all three children  
✅ You are responsible for them and they live with you full-time  
✅ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for them  
✅ They are living normally at home (not in hospital, care, or fostered)  
✅ The local council is not paying towards their care  

### Next Steps - How to Claim:

1. **Apply online or by post** using form CH2
2. **Provide proof of identity** for yourself and each child
3. **Provide birth certificates** (or adoption certificates if applicable) for Alex, Blake, and Charlie

### Payment Information:

- Payments are usually made **every 4 weeks** (or weekly if you're a single parent or receiving certain benefits)
- Current rates depend on whether this is your eldest/only child or additional children

### Important Note - High Income Child Benefit Charge:

If you or your partner earn over £60,000 per year, you may need to pay a tax charge on some or all of the Child Benefit. You can still claim and choose whether to receive payments or not.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about claiming Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex (age 3) meets all eligibility criteria: (1) Under 16 years old, (2) Not working, (3) Not receiving benefits in their own right, (4) Not in an apprenticeship, (5) Lives with the claimant in the UK, (6) The claimant is responsible for Alex, (7) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, (8) Not in hospital, care, or fostered, (9) Local council is not paying towards accommodation or maintenance. Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake (age 8) meets all eligibility criteria: (1) Under 16 years old, (2) Not working, (3) Not receiving benefits in their own right, (4) Not in an apprenticeship, (5) Lives with the claimant in the UK, (6) The claimant is responsible for Blake, (7) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake, (8) Not in hospital, care, or fostered, (9) Local council is not paying towards accommodation or maintenance. Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Blake.

Charlie (age 2) meets all eligibility criteria: (1) Under 16 years old, (2) Not working, (3) Not receiving benefits in their own right, (4) Not in an apprenticeship, (5) Lives with the claimant in the UK, (6) The claimant is responsible for Charlie, (7) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Charlie, (8) Not in hospital, care, or fostered, (9) Local council is not paying towards accommodation or maintenance. Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Charlie.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has three children (Alex age 3, Blake age 8, and Charlie age 2) all living with them full-time in England. All three children are under 16, not working, not receiving benefits in their own right, and not in any apprenticeships. The claimant lives in the UK and is responsible for all three children who live with them. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for any of the children, none are in hospital/care/fostered, and the local council is not paying towards their accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility criteria are met for all three children, making the claimant eligible for Child Benefit for Alex, Blake, and Charlie.
